basics.name: Alonso Caballero
basics.label: Front-End Web Developer | UX/UI Focused
basics.summary: I'm a Front-End Developer with over 10 years of experience building fast, optimized, and user-centered web experiences. I bridge the gap between design, business goals, and clean code — leveraging React, Vue, or Astro when scalability matters, and Webflow, WordPress, or Shopify when speed-to-market is the priority. I work with data, technical SEO, and A/B testing (VWO) to continuously improve what already exists, not just ship what's new.

work.name: Viajes el Corte Inglés México
work.summary: At Viajes el Corte Inglés México, I joined the team to strengthen their digital presence by developing promotional landing pages on Magento, managing the full creation and update lifecycle to optimize user experience. I collaborated closely with the UX team, contributing to the redesign of the website and the transition to Magento 2 — improving critical touchpoints such as the homepage and checkout flow using PHP, HTML5, CSS3, Bootstrap, JavaScript, and jQuery. I later took ownership of the shift toward a headless architecture, adopting React.js, Next.js, and TailwindCSS to build scalable, performant components and prototypes that laid the foundation for a more modern and agile digital platform.
highlights: Developed high-converting landing pages and email marketing campaigns that directly increased online bookings.
Optimized website performance, resulting in a measurably improved user experience throughout the purchase funnel.
Led the front-end migration from Magento to a headless architecture, significantly improving site load times and scalability.

work.name: GAIA Design
work.summary: At GAIA Design, one of Mexico's leading furniture and home decor e-commerce brands, I played a key role in evolving their digital storefront. I developed and maintained promotional landing pages on Magento, working hand-in-hand with the UX team to improve the overall shopping experience. I contributed to the complete rebuild of the website and the migration to Magento 2, enhancing the homepage and checkout flow using PHP, HTML5, CSS3, Bootstrap, JavaScript, and jQuery. As the platform matured, I transitioned into a headless architecture role, developing reusable components and prototypes with React.js, Next.js, and TailwindCSS — enabling a more scalable, maintainable, and high-performance e-commerce experience.
highlights: Built and maintained promotional web pages and email marketing campaigns that drove measurable increases in online sales.
Improved website performance and front-end responsiveness, enhancing the end-to-end user purchase experience.
Successfully led the front-end migration from Magento to a headless stack, boosting site speed and long-term scalability.

work.name: Sales Ready Marketing
work.summary: As a Webflow Developer and Website Content Manager, I took full ownership of the design, development, and ongoing management of client websites. Using Webflow's visual development environment, I delivered polished, fully functional, and SEO-ready sites with fast turnaround times — bridging the gap between marketing strategy and technical execution for a B2B-focused marketing agency.

work.name: Interjet
work.summary: As a Front-End Developer within Interjet's UX team, I was responsible for building and maintaining responsive, visually compelling web interfaces that enhanced the digital experience for millions of travelers. I worked extensively with HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, and Bootstrap 3 to craft user-friendly layouts and interaction patterns. In addition to front-end development, I contributed to digital marketing initiatives by designing and implementing email marketing campaigns using Oracle Responsys — creating targeted, engaging communications that drove higher audience interaction and conversion rates.

work.name: Liverpool
work.summary: At Liverpool, one of Mexico's largest retail chains, I contributed to the growth and modernization of their e-commerce platform. I played an active role in the migration to Oracle BCC & ATG Web Commerce, improving the management and scalability of the online store. I collaborated with international teams to enhance the checkout process and developed branded landing pages for key retail partners. I also contributed to the successful launch of multi-brand sites including Williams-Sonoma, Pottery Barn, and West Elm — applying UX/UI best practices throughout to deliver seamless, high-quality digital shopping experiences.

work.name: CTIN (Telmex Center for Technology & Innovation)
work.summary: During my internship at CTIN, I gained hands-on experience across multiple areas of web development. I worked with HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, and jQuery to build interactive and visually engaging websites. I also had the opportunity to facilitate digital skills workshops at Aldea Digital TELMEX, sharing knowledge and helping participants explore opportunities in technology and innovation. This experience was foundational in shaping my technical mindset and my passion for building impactful digital products.

work.name: Freelance
work.summary: As an independent Front-End Developer, I've partnered with clients across industries to design and build high-impact websites tailored to their specific goals. My stack spans HTML5, CSS3, Bootstrap, JavaScript frameworks, WordPress, and Joomla — allowing me to deliver both static and dynamic web experiences with a strong emphasis on performance, usability, and visual quality. I take pride in understanding each client's business needs and translating them into clean, maintainable, and effective digital solutions.
